title: eriolaena polyandra
intro: Eriolaena polyandra is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family, and is native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been shown to possess potential medicinal benefits. Some of the health benefits associated with Eriolaena polyandra include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Eriolaena polyandra is often prepared as a decoction by boiling its roots in water to create a medicinal tea or infusion, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
